patch.value: Split metadata cache into light + collections tiers to cut list hot-path JSON parse

On the 873-item dev repo loadCache JSON-parses a 4.98MB metadata-cache.json on every list/stats/deps/activity/calendar. Most bytes are heavy collection fields (comments alone=237KB for pm-rnpb). pm list compact (hot path) only needs id/status/type/title. Follow-on to pm-4r5t body-split: store comments/notes/learnings/files/tests/test_runs/docs in a separate lazily-loaded collections cache; the always-loaded light cache shrinks ~10x, JSON.parse 35ms->~3ms. Audit: light-only=activity/stats/deps/close/test-all/calendar; heavy=list(full/heavy-fields)/validate/health(drift hash)/search-fallback/linked-artifacts(--audit)/SDK.
Split metadata cache into light + lazily-loaded collections tiers; hot list/stats/deps/activity/calendar/close/test-all paths use listAllFrontMatterLight and skip the heavy collections cache.
